text: he dropped his hand to his side. Time crept past. Distantly, the buzz of the bots working the park drifted into the silence . “Can you?” he finally asked . “ Can’t you ?” Ronin frowned. Had she gone too far? He hadn’t touched her yet, but she’d seen him moving his fingers as though he wanted to more than once. Bots did what they said, and he said he wouldn’t … But Ronin wasn’t like other bots . She forced that thought aside, before it led her back to the dream . “I can, but I limit my actions by considering the potential consequences. For example.” He crossed the room toward her, boots thumping on the floor — she knew the bastard snuck around on purpose. He stopped beside the table . This would be it. He’d grab hold of her and make her hate bots all over again, because of a fucking table. But that’s what she wanted, right? To have her hate back, to end her conflicting emotions ? “ What are — ” Before she could finish, he grasped the edge of the table with one hand and tipped it up. Lara’s stomach lurched as she was lifted. She pushed away from the table and, somehow, got her feet beneath her . She spun to face him. “The hell did you do that for ?” He eased the table back onto its legs. “Because I can do whatever the hell I want .” “Oh, you wanna play it that way , huh ?” She stalked across the room, fists balled at her sides, and stopped beside the chair. Turning, she met his gaze and kicked the chair over. The sound was enormous in the relative quiet . Ronin didn’t flinch, didn’t blink; he just stared . “Do you feel better now?” he asked, and she hated the genuine curiosity in his voice . “I’m bored .” “I would appreciate it if, in the future, you tell me such things sooner. I’d rather spare the furniture such abuse, when possible .” “I’m sure the chair will survive .” “How do we relieve your boredom ?” The image of his hand under her shirt, slowly sliding up to her breasts, blasted to the forefront of her mind. She forced it away as quickly as it came. “I don’t know. I just need something to do. I’m used to scavenging during the day, or helping the neighbors with repairs. Not just…sitting around !” “Is it more bearable if you stand ?” It took a moment, but when it hit her, she narrowed her eyes at him. “Smart ass. Why is it you only have a sense of humor sometimes ?” “Memory damage. Leaves me terribly inconsistent .” Lara smirked. “You can say that again. Not literally !” He snapped his mouth shut . “I just need something to do, Ronin. Something to keep me busy. Teach me something, anything. Just so I’m not counting the minutes .” “All right,” he said, looking toward the window. “Tables are for sitting at , not on . Have you learned something ?” “Yeah. Will I obey? Probably not. I’m serious, Ronin. I need something to do. I don’t…I don’t have a purpose here .” He raised a hand and scratched his cheek, eyes still on the window. “I’ll see what I can find .” “There’s nothing here. I’ve looked .” “I mean when I go out again. I’m leaving tomorrow. Into the Dust .” Lara straightened, brows rising. “I can go with you! We’ll look for Tabitha together .” “No,” he replied, a sharp edge in his voice that she hadn’t heard from him before. He settled his eyes on her. “It’s too dangerous , Lara .” “I’ve scavenged for most of my life. I know the dangers .” “You know nothing of the dangers out there. Have you ever been beyond the ruins of Cheyenne ?” She threw her arms out in frustration. “You can’t expect me to just stay here!” Pacing, she shook her head. “How long are you gonna be gone ?” “Depends on what I find. If I push it, maybe two or three weeks .” “ What ? Tell me you’re just testing out that sense of humor. For the record, that’s not funny. I am not gonna stay here for three weeks by myself with nothing to do !” He turned his body toward her. It was an eerie, inhuman movement; his head remained fixed in place while the rest of him shifted. “I’ll make sure there’s plenty of — ” “I’m not kidding!” Cold dread clawed up her spine. She’d be trapped. Alone. Surrounded by bots. “You can’t leave me here that long .” His brow furrowed. “What’s an acceptable timeframe for you?” he asked, after a long silence . “A day. Maybe two. Though I don’t understand why you can’t just take me along .” He shook his head. The motions stopped too abruptly at each side, accelerated too quickly, giving it an unnatural look. “It’s too dangerous. The first storm without shelter…” He lifted his hands to waist-level, dropped his gaze to them, and curled his fingers. “Three days. There’s little chance of me getting far enough to find anything of value in less time than that .” She stared at him, clenching her jaw. “ Damn you .” “I have to uphold my part of the bargain. That means I need to earn credits. I can only do that with scrap.” He stuffed his hands into his pockets . “So take me back home, and the deal can be done .” What response could she expect from that? What response did she want ? “No,” he said too quickly, unexpected finality in his tone . “Why? What do you — ” Someone — or something — knocked on the front door. Lara snapped her mouth shut. She and Ronin both turned their heads to look at it. When their eyes met again,
